title: Add thumbnails CLI command to cli.py
description: Register the `thumbnails` subcommand in pypptx/cli.py, wiring up --output PREFIX (default: thumbnails) and --cols N (default: 3, max: 6) options and delegating to generate_thumbnails(). Print JSON {"files": [...]} to stdout by default; with --plain print one path per line.
acceptance_criteria: `pypptx thumbnails --help` shows usage with --output and --cols options
`pypptx thumbnails deck.pptx --plain` prints one file path per line
--cols defaults to 3 and rejects values greater than 6

handoff_summary.completed: Registered `thumbnails` CLI command in `pypptx/cli.py` with all required options and output modes.
handoff_summary.risks: None identified. `generate_thumbnails()` already existed and was stable; this bead only adds the CLI binding.
handoff_summary.next_action: Hand off to tester agent to verify acceptance criteria against a real .pptx fixture.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: None. Only `pypptx/cli.py` was modified; no sibling beads claim this file.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: Used `tempfile.TemporaryDirectory` as context manager so intermediate files are always cleaned up. `--cols` validation done before `check_dependencies()` to fail fast on bad input without triggering dependency checks.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: Tester should verify: JSON output shape, --plain one-path-per-line, --cols 7 rejected, --output prefix applied to output filenames, missing dependencies produce actionable error messages.

conflict_risks: None. Only `pypptx/cli.py` was modified; no sibling beads claim this file.

execution_history.summary: Added `thumbnails` subcommand to `pypptx/cli.py`. The command wires up `--output PREFIX` (default: thumbnails), `--cols N` (default: 3, max: 6), and `--plain` flag, delegating to `generate_thumbnails()` via a temporary directory. JSON output is `{"files": [...]}` by default; `--plain` prints one path per line. `--cols > 6` exits with an error. `check_dependencies()` is called before any file I/O. Syntax verified with `py_compile`; `--help` output confirmed correct.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your `Python` changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: `python -m py_compile`.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not even a targeted `pytest` run or any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
